I suspect that after Disney bought fox it had to do with placing the movie in a slot such that it did not conflict with any of the MCU films that were coming out.  It would be a waste of a movie if they sandwiched it in between Black Panther, Infinity War, Captain Marvel, Guardians of the Galaxy, Endgame, etc. 

This is because those MCU films were part of a continuing story that people were highly invested in.  By putting the New Mutants in between any one of those MCU films, would've proved disastrous for the movie.

The delay was necessary and was the right call.

The New Mutants will be the first superhero movie of 2020 whereby they don't have to worry about concerning themselves about other blockbuster MCU films.

Besides, this could be a blessing in disguise for the New Mutants.  If the movie turns out to be a hit or at least an ok movie, this could be the introduction of Mutants into the MCU.  At some point, the old X-Men characters that everyone are familiar with could then trickle into the MCU.

However, Disney did not do any reshoots for this film.  This movie is going as-is.  Besides, this is a win-win for Disney.  My understanding is that the New Mutants budget was around $40 million dollars.  The New Mutants will probably make that back in the opening weekend, if not, more.

In my opinion, I think this is going to be a sleeper hit.

-Maisie Williams is Rahne Sinclair a.k.a. Wolfsbane, whose religious upbringing is making it difficult for her to reconcile her beliefs with her ability to turn into a wolf. In the comics, she was an orphan raised by an abusive pastor until she was rescued (and adopted) by Moira MacTaggert.


-Charlie Heaton plays Sam Guthrie a.k.a. Cannonball, a mutant who has the ability of flight. Of course, Cannonball doesn’t just fly, he has the ability to create an impenetrable force field around himself while he’s propelling himself through the air.


-Henry Zaga is Roberto da Costa a.k.a. Sunspot, who has the ability to absorb and manipulate solar energy.


-Blu Hunt is Danielle Moonstar a.k.a. Mirage, a mutant with psyonic powers that enable her to create illusions drawing from a person’s fear or desires. In the comics she has many more powers, including telepathy, energy manipulation and even superhuman strength.
